festivals.name: Día de los Muertos
festivals.description: Mexico's most iconic festival honors the dead with marigold-covered altars, skull makeup, and candlelit cemetery vigils.

festivals.name: Guelaguetza
festivals.description: Oaxaca's great indigenous festival showcases music, dances, and costumes from eight distinct ethnic communities.

destinations.name: Mexico City
destinations.description: One of the Americas' greatest cities layers Aztec ruins under Spanish baroque cathedrals with world-class museums.

destinations.name: Oaxaca
destinations.description: Mexico's culinary and cultural capital with the best mole, mezcal, and some of the country's finest artisan markets.

destinations.name: Chichén Itzá
destinations.description: One of the New Seven Wonders of the World, the Maya city's pyramid El Castillo casts a serpent shadow at equinox.

destinations.name: Palenque
destinations.description: Maya jungle city of extraordinary carved relief panels, hidden temples, and Lord Pakal's elaborate tomb.

destinations.name: Copper Canyon (Barrancas del Cobre)
destinations.description: Four times larger than the Grand Canyon, traversed by the dramatic Chepe train through Tarahumara indigenous territory.

destinations.name: Guanajuato
destinations.description: A UNESCO World Heritage colonial city of colorful hillside houses connected by underground tunnels and narrow alleys.

destinations.name: Teotihuacán
destinations.description: The Pyramids of the Sun and Moon in the City of the Gods were built by an unknown civilization 2,000 years ago.

destinations.name: San Cristóbal de las Casas
destinations.description: A cool highland colonial town surrounded by indigenous Tzotzil and Tzeltal Maya communities and cloud forest.
